heading: Update and Rebuild an Existing MongoDB Search Index
body:
Some MongoDB Search features require that you update the index. To update
your index, you can take one of the following actions:

- Edit the index definition using the steps in
  [Changes](ref-edit-index).

- Do the following steps to opt-in for an index update from the
  Atlas UI:

> **Important:** The process for updating the MongoDB Search index requires time and resources.
> For the index update to complete successfully, allocate free disk space
> equal to 125% of your current index's disk space.

WARNING: If an index requires an update to enable a new feature,
the Atlas UI shows a warning icon next to the index name.

- In the Index Overview page, click Update in the banner.

- Click Update Index in the confirmation window.

  While your index is updating, you can continue to query your
  collection using your previous index.
